title: crotalaria laevigata
intro: Crotalaria laevigata, also known as the Smooth Rattlebox, is a plant species in the legume family (Fabaceae) native to tropical Africa. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional African medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The leaves and roots of the plant have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, offering potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and fighting infections. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and the leaves are sometimes used in herbal remedies, such as teas and poultices.
